summary: 2026-07-01 14:16:02 PDT - Workflows are structurally aligned on triggers, minimal permissions, full-SHA checkout pin, PR base..head gitleaks range, and weekly cron. I found docs overclaiming AC4 branch protection as already active even though this review scope says AC4 is still Phase 2. I could not independently recompute the gitleaks release asset hash because outbound curl cannot resolve github.com here; the workflow does include checksum verification logic.

findings.title: README overclaims Phase 2 branch protection as active
findings.claim: The requested scope says AC4 branch protection is intentionally Phase 2 and not yet applied, but the README states server-side branch protection is "now active" and says main already requires the CI gate, PR merges, and admin enforcement. That makes the docs claim a security backstop that is not yet in place.
findings.suggestion: Until the Phase 2 gh-api protection read-back is complete, rewrite this section to say CI and the verified settings are in place, while branch protection is pending Phase 2; only state that main is protected after AC4 is actually applied and verified.

findings.title: ARCHITECTURE overclaims required branch protection
findings.claim: The architecture doc says branch protection is "now active" and later says main already requires the gate, PR merges, and enforce_admins=true. Per the requested review scope, AC4 is still Phase 2, so this is a false current-state claim rather than a description of implemented behavior.
findings.suggestion: Change the current-state wording to reflect that CI exists and AC4 branch protection will be configured in Phase 2 after the observed check context is used; move the "required status check" and enforce_admins claims to post-AC4 wording.
